name: Na'im ibn Ajlan
title: Khazraji Poet
shortDescription: A renowned poet and warrior of the Khazraj tribe who rushed to the Imam's aid on Ashura and was martyred in the first attack.
fullStory:
Na'im ibn Ajlan al-Ansari al-Khazraji was a sincere Shia from Kufa and a well-known poet and warrior of the Khazraj tribe. His brothers were also devoted companions of Amir al-Mu'minin Ali (AS) in previous battles.

When the news of Imam Hussain's (AS) movement to Karbala spread in Kufa, Na'im, having a heart full of love for the Ahl al-Bayt, waited for an opportunity to join him. He bravely passed through Ibn Ziyad's soldiers in Kufa and reached the Hussaini caravan in Karbala before the roads were completely closed.

On the day of Ashura, Na'im stood beside the Master of Martyrs with an epic spirit and indescribable bravery. As a poet, he boosted the morale of the Imam's companions and degraded the enemy with his verses and war poetry.

With the start of the cowardly all-out attack by Umar Sa'd's 30,000-strong army on the morning of Ashura, Na'im ibn Ajlan drew his sword and manfully defended the Imam's tents. In this fierce battle, he fought shoulder to shoulder with the Imam's other loyal companions.

Finally, this Khazraji poet and warrior, after killing a number of enemies, was martyred by arrows in that very first attack and achieved a blessed end.
quoteArabic: السَّلامُ عَلى نُعَيْمِ بْنِ عَجْلانَ الأَنْصارِيِّ...
quote: (From Ziyarat Nahiya:) Peace be upon Na'im ibn Ajlan al-Ansari.
